title: Market Brief — Tuesday, June 2, 2026
summary: GPU supply is loosening fast across every class. Util-avail stands at 22% for A100, 23% for H100, 35% for H200, and 50% for B200, meaning only A100 and H100 remain near the scarcity threshold while newer silicon is approaching oversupply. H100 spot crashed 22.4% day-over-day to $2.80/hr, the largest single-day move in the dataset, signaling demand destruction or capacity dumps from hyperscalers.
paragraphs: GPU supply is loosening fast across every class. Util-avail stands at 22% for A100, 23% for H100, 35% for H200, and 50% for B200, meaning only A100 and H100 remain near the scarcity threshold while newer silicon is approaching oversupply. H100 spot crashed 22.4% day-over-day to $2.80/hr, the largest single-day move in the dataset, signaling demand destruction or capacity dumps from hyperscalers.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $10.21/M tokens, yet DeepSeek V3.2 sells output at $0.34/M and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M, both burning cash at staggering scale. OpenAI grades Neutral, sustaining margin only at premium tiers. Anthropic grades Neutral, with Haiku at $5/M output clearing TCO. DeepSeek and Meta grade Burning Cash, subsidizing adoption with every token served.

B200 at 50% util-avail and 111.9% annualized price volatility is overvalued for anyone locking in contracts today. A100 at $1.00/hr spot and 78.3% utilization is the undervalued workhorse for cost-sensitive inference, buy spot A100 and sell into premium-tier API margins at 89% gross.
bullets: 50% of B200 compute capacity sits idle by Ornn utilization, the loosest GPU class tracked.
22% util-avail on A100 makes it the tightest compute class despite being the oldest silicon.
$2.80/hr H100 spot is down 22.4% in one day, the sharpest drop across all GPUs.
21.78 on the LLMflation index means frontier output costs have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.34/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a loss of roughly $9.87/M tokens against TCO-adjusted H100 cost.
199.4% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest GPU to hold on contract.
